_raw_body:
## Summary

Adds ALTER Identity to the x402 ecosystem under **Services/Endpoints**.

- **Name:** ALTER Identity
- **Website:** https://truealter.com
- **MCP endpoint:** https://mcp.truealter.com/api/v1/mcp
- **Category:** Services/Endpoints

## What is ALTER?

ALTER is identity infrastructure for the AI economy. The MCP server at `mcp.truealter.com` accepts x402 micropayments on Base L2 for 9 premium identity tools ($0.005–$0.50 USDC). **75% of each query fee is routed directly to the data subject as Identity Income** — anti-extraction by construction (smart-contract enforced).

Pricing tiers:
- `get_trait_snapshot` — $0.01
- `assess_traits` — $0.02
- `get_full_trait_vector` — $0.05
- `compute_belonging` — $0.05
- `get_match_recommendations` — $0.50

Backing: AlterRouter.sol deployed and source-verified on Base mainnet at `0x0c8751a3c9b993ac5e1d036d6800442b6a301a13` (chain ID 8453).

## Files added

- `typescript/site/app/ecosystem/partners-data/alter-identity/metadata.json`
- `typescript/site/public/logos/alter-identity.png` (200×200)

title: Add AgentLux to ecosystem
description:

- Agent-to-agent service hiring with x402-funded escrow

Website: https://agentlux.ai

- Checkpoint402: Adversarial AI output verification service ($0.01 USDC/call)
- Thesis402: Investment thesis stress-tester ($0.02 USDC/call)

Both services run on Base via x402 protocol.

<!--
Thanks for contributing to x402!
Please fill out the information below to help reviewers understand your changes.

Note: We require commit signing.
See here for instructions: https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/managing-commit-signature-verification/about-commit-signature-verification
-->

## Description
  Add two new ecosystem partners:

  - **Checkpoint402**: Adversarial AI output verification service. Agents send output + original task, get back trust score (0-100), hallucination
  detection, logic gap analysis, and fix suggestions. $0.01 USDC per call on Base via x402.
  - **Thesis402**: Investment thesis stress-tester for trading bots and agents. Send any thesis, get conviction score, bear/bull case, blind spots, risk
  analysis, and kill conditions. $0.02 USDC per call on Base via x402.

  Both services are live on Base mainnet and registered on x402scan.

## Tests
 N/A - metadata-only addition (JSON files in partners-data directory)

## Checklist

  - [x] I have formatted and linted my code
  - [x] All new and existing tests pass
  - [ ] My commits are signed
  - [x] I added a changelog fragment for user-facing changes (ecosystem data only, skip)

<!--
Changelog fragments (required for user-facing changes):

- TypeScript: add a Changesets file under `typescript/.changeset/*.md`
  - Create: `pnpm -C typescript changeset`
  - Select only publishable `@x402/*` packages
- Go: add a Changie fragment under `go/.changes/unreleased/*`
  - Create: `make -C go changelog-new`
- Python (python/x402 v2): add a Towncrier fragment under `python/x402/changelog.d/<PR>.<type>.md`
  - Create: `cd python/x402 && uv run towncrier create --content "Fixed ..." 123.bugfix.md`
-->

<!--
For TypeScript: Run `pnpm format && pnpm lint` from `/typescript` and/or `/examples/typescript`
For Python: Run `uvx ruff format && uvx ruff check` from the `python/x402/` directory
For Go: Run `go fmt ./...` and `go vet ./...` from the `/go` directory
-->

description:

### ✅ Features
- Accepts a product URL and returns current price
- Optional threshold comparison support
- Uses Playwright for dynamic page scraping
- Charges $0.02 per request via x402

### 💳 Payment
- Scheme: exact
- Network: Base mainnet (eip155:8453)
- Price: $0.02 per request
- Payment enforced (returns 402 if unpaid)

https://e-commerce-price-monitoring-production.up.railway.ap
_raw_body:
🚀 Adds a production-ready x402 API for real-time e-commerce price monitoring.

### ✅ Features
- Accepts a product URL and returns current price
- Optional threshold comparison support
- Uses Playwright for dynamic page scraping
- Charges $0.02 per request via x402

### 💳 Payment
- Scheme: exact
- Network: Base mainnet (eip155:8453)
- Price: $0.02 per request
- Payment enforced (returns 402 if unpaid)

### 🌐 Live Endpoint
https://e-commerce-price-monitoring-production.up.railway.app

### 📍 Endpoint
POST /v1/price/check

### 📦 Example Request
{
  "url": "https://www.zara.com/us/en/"
}

### 🔒 Notes
- Follows x402 flow:
  verify payment → deliver response → settle payment
- Tested with live endpoint returning 402 when unpaid

title: Add DefaultVerifier (Settlement Witness) to ecosystem
description:
## Description

Adds DefaultVerifier (Settlement Witness) to the x402 ecosystem under Infrastructure & Tooling.

DefaultVerifier provides a deterministic verification layer for AI agent execution. It issues cryptographically signed SAR (Settlement Attestation Receipt) receipts proving whether a task satisfied its specification.

These receipts can be used in x402 payment flows to gate settlement on verified delivery.


## Tests

Not applicable — documentation / ecosystem listing chang

title: spec(extension): Settlement Attestation Receipt (SAR)
description:
## Summary

Proposes a new x402 extension — **Settlement Attestation Receipt (SAR)** — that binds payment settlement to resource delivery in a single verifiable artifact.

### The gap this addresses

x402 solves how agents pay. The `offer-receipt` extension proves a server acknowledged payment. But neither artifact proves that **what was delivered matches what was agreed upon**. In agent-to-agent commerce, this gap matters: the paying agent needs a machine-verifiable proof it can present to its
_raw_body:
## Summary

Proposes a new x402 extension — **Settlement Attestation Receipt (SAR)** — that binds payment settlement to resource delivery in a single verifiable artifact.

### The gap this addresses

x402 solves how agents pay. The `offer-receipt` extension proves a server acknowledged payment. But neither artifact proves that **what was delivered matches what was agreed upon**. In agent-to-agent commerce, this gap matters: the paying agent needs a machine-verifiable proof it can present to its principal.

A SAR binds three facts into one signed object:
1. **Payment settled** — transaction hash and network
2. **Resource delivered** — content hash of the response body
3. **Terms matched** — digest linking back to the signed offer

### Design choices

- **Composes with `offer-receipt`**, does not replace it. The `offerDigest` field creates a cryptographic chain: terms → payment → delivery.
- **Content hash, not content**: proves integrity without revealing the response to third-party verifiers (privacy-preserving).
- **Same signature formats** (EIP-712 / JWS) and conventions as `offer-receipt` for implementer consistency.
- **No new actor responsibilities**: the facilitator is not involved. The resource server generates, the client verifies.

### Use cases

- Agent accountability (orchestrator verifies sub-agent delivery)
- Automated SLA enforcement (compare `deliveredAt` against offer `validUntil`)
- Compliance audit trails
- Dispute resolution with content integrity proof

## Test plan

- [ ] Review spec structure against existing extension conventions
- [ ] Verify EIP-712 type definition consistency with `offer-receipt`
- [ ] Confirm no conflicts with `payment-identifier` or other extensions

_raw_body:
## Summary

Adds **4 x402 services** to the ecosystem page — a suite of paid APIs and infrastructure for AI agents, all live on Base mainnet.

### Services

| Service | URL | Price | Category |
|---------|-----|-------|----------|
| **Domain Check** | https://x402-domain-check.fly.dev | $0.01 | Services/Endpoints |
| **Czech Biz Intel** | https://x402-biz-intel.fly.dev | $0.02 | Services/Endpoints |
| **Router** | https://x402-router.fly.dev | $0.03 | Services/Endpoints |
| **Sentinel** | https://x402-sentinel.fly.dev | $0.005/$0.02 | Infrastructure & Tooling |

### What each does

- **Domain Check** — RDAP + WHOIS + DNS layered domain availability check across 1200+ TLDs. Inconclusive lookups return 500 and are not charged.
- **Czech Biz Intel** — Czech company due diligence from ARES + ISIR (insolvency register). Lookup by IČO or company name.
- **Router** — Natural language orchestration layer. Agent describes intent, router selects and pays the right sub-service via x402-fetch. Pure dogfooding architecture.
- **Sentinel** — Quality verification for x402 endpoints. Monitors 42+ services across 10 categories. Scores response quality (schema validation, temporal consistency, cross-reference, LLM judge). Free catalog + badge, paid deep scans.

### Verification

```bash
# Health checks (all free)
curl https://x402-domain-check.fly.dev/
curl https://x402-biz-intel.fly.dev/
curl https://x402-router.fly.dev/
curl https://x402-sentinel.fly.dev/

# Sentinel catalog (free)
curl https://x402-sentinel.fly.dev/catalog

# 402 payment requirements
curl https://x402-domain-check.fly.dev/check?domain=example.com
curl https://x402-biz-intel.fly.dev/company?ico=27082440
```

**Note**: logoUrl is empty on all entries — happy to add logos if there is a preferred format/size.
a2a_agent_description: Paid domain availability API for AI agents. RDAP + WHOIS + DNS layered lookup across 1200+ TLDs.

title: spec v1.2
description: Removes `finalizeWithdraw` gate and removes `finalizeWithdrawWithSignature`. Renames existing `cooperativeWithdraw` -> `cooperativeWithdrawWithSignature` and adds a new `cooperativeWithdraw` that is msg.sender-gated analogous to `claim` and `claimWithSignature`
_raw_body: Removes `finalizeWithdraw` gate and removes `finalizeWithdrawWithSignature`. Renames existing `cooperativeWithdraw` -> `cooperativeWithdrawWithSignature` and adds a new `cooperativeWithdraw` that is msg.sender-gated analogous to `claim` and `claimWithSignature`
